content: The half-life period of a radioactive element '$$X$$' is same as the mean life time of another radioactive element '$$Y$$'. Initially, both of them have the same number of atoms, then

options.content: both of them will decay at same decay constant

explanation:
$$\therefore$$ Rate of decay $$=\frac{d N}{d t}=-\lambda N$$

where, $$N=$$ original number of atom

$$\lambda=$$ decay constant

Half-life, $$t_{1 / 2}=\frac{\ln 2}{\lambda}=\frac{0.693}{\lambda}$$

Mean time, $$\tau=\frac{1}{\lambda}$$

Given, $$\left(t_{1 / 2}\right)_x=\tau_y$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \left(\frac{0.693}{\lambda}\right)_X =\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}\right)_Y \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \lambda_X =0.693 \times \lambda_Y \\ & \therefore \quad \lambda_Y >\lambda_X \end{aligned}$$

Higher the value of decay constant faster the decay.

$$\therefore Y$$ will decay faster.

content: Which of the following isotopes is used to treat cancer?
explanation: Cobalt-60 is used to treat cancer because it is a synthetic radioactive isotope of cobalt and emits gamma rays which are used to treat cancer.
